notes: Esync must be disabled. put  "PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 %command%" in launch option of game without quotes.

notes: Runs with PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 %command%. Played one Chapter with Steamcontroller. Noticeable lower Framerate than in Windows but very playable if details are set to medium instead of very high.
title: Thief

notes: Works out of the box when Esync is configured properly on your system
title: Thief

notes: A tweak is required to /etc/security/limits.conf due to what seems to be some kind of stack overflow. Adding "*                hard    nofile          1048576" solves this, and the game runs without any major issues. Selecting "use 32 bit binary" in the launch menu also appears to be required.
title: Thief

title: Thief

notes: It works out of the box.
title: Thief

notes: PROTON_USE_WINED3D=1 required to lunch. The game kind of works, but there are various minor rendering issues. One of them is a pickup notification thingy, that makes everything much darker when it shows up. One needs to move a bit for the screen go back to normal brightness. One can disable these notifications in HUD settings tho, and that makes the game almost perfect. There are few more issues, the screen goes much brighter and have flat shading, when interacting with new locations or interacting with some NPC and cutscenes, and goes back to correct brightness after interaction is over. Really messes up with immersion. There is also one or two minor rendering artifacts in the benchmark, but as far as I know the same artifacts show on Windows. Framerate in game is acceptable, and reasonably smooth. But I think it is lower than in Windows. Benchmark with all settings a high/very high, ran at 1920x1200, gives me only average 14.4 FPS, where I am certain I should be getting about 90-100 fps (Windows with Mantle). So performance is poor.
title: Thief

notes: Works out of the box.
title: Thief

notes: Worked out of the box, no issues found.
title: Thief

notes: After the tweak game runs without a problem. Some expected stuttering in the beginning of levels (shader caching). Benchmark was 19 FPS min 49 FPS avg 86 FPS max. Resolution 3440x1440, maxed out except SSAA and FXAA. To prevent the game from crashing you need to edit "/etc/security/limits.conf" and add this line "* hard nofile 1048576". If you use a distro with systemd you need to additionally add this line "DefaultLimitNOFILE=1024:1048576" into these two files "/etc/systemd/system.conf" and "/etc/systemd/user.conf"
title: Thief

notes: Max settings on 2560x1440, min/avg fps 50/75 in benchmark, no launch options (those actually make it worse)
title: Thief

notes:
No launch options, followed the instructions here to ensure that esync works properly https://github.com/zfigura/wine/blob/esync/README.esync
Performance in benchmark: Min. FPS: 5.1, Max. FPS: 98.5, Avg. FPS: 51.6
